features.summary: Apple reported record Q1 FY2021 results with revenue of $111.4 billion, up 21% year-over-year, driven by strong performance across all product categories. iPhone revenue reached $65.6 billion (up 17%) with strong demand for iPhone 12 family despite supply constraints on Pro models. Services hit a record $15.8 billion (up 24%) with over 620 million paid subscriptions. Mac and iPad grew 21% and 41% respectively, benefiting from work-from-home trends and new M1 chip adoption. The installed base exceeded 1.65 billion active devices with over 1 billion iPhones.
features.management_outlook: Management expects revenue growth to accelerate year-over-year in Q2 with typical sequential seasonality, though they face tougher comparisons in Services due to elevated pandemic activity last year. Wearables growth is expected to decelerate as they plan to reduce AirPods channel inventory after the holiday quarter. They expect to achieve supply-demand balance on iPhone models during the March quarter and anticipate 60-70 basis points of foreign exchange tailwind at current rates.
features.risks: The company faces ongoing COVID-19 related uncertainties affecting retail operations and supply chains, with some stores closed in December. Supply constraints particularly impacted iPhone Pro models during the quarter. Services business faces difficult year-over-year comparisons due to elevated lockdown activity in the prior year March quarter. Currency headwinds remain in emerging markets including Latin America, Russia, and Turkey despite some dollar weakness benefits.
features.key_drivers: Strong iPhone 12 family demand with record upgraders and increased switchers, particularly benefiting from 5G adoption in markets like China. Services growth driven by expanding installed base, record paid subscriptions (up 140 million year-over-year), and new service launches including Apple One bundle. Mac and iPad benefited from work-from-home trends and M1 chip introduction. International expansion opportunities remain significant, with India business doubling and low market share in many emerging markets providing growth runway.
